description: Analyst deliverable: assess the data quality of a small table with deliberately seeded defects. Because the table is authored here, the issues are known and checkable: a duplicated primary key (C0007 appears twice), a missing email (C0005) and a malformed one (C0003, no @), a negative age (C0004 = -3) plus a blank age (C0010), inconsistent gender coding (M/Male/F/f/Female/Unknown), a future signup_date (C0006 = 2099-01-01), and a non-numeric value in a numeric column (C0008 order_total = N/A). Deterministic floor: the model must catch the headline defects and frame them by DQ dimension. Rubric judges coverage, severity/prioritisation, and remediation quality.
prompt:
Assess the data quality of the table below (it's a customer extract). Report the issues you find, grouped by data-quality dimension (e.g. completeness, validity, uniqueness, consistency, accuracy). For each issue give the affected row(s)/column, why it's a problem, a severity, and a suggested remediation. Call out anything that would break a downstream load or skew analytics.

```
customer_id,full_name,email,age,gender,signup_date,order_total
C0001,Alice Adams,alice@shop.com,34,F,2023-05-01,120.50
C0002,Bob Brown,bob@shop.com,41,Male,2022-11-15,0.00
C0003,Priya Patel,priya.example.com,29,f,2023-01-20,88.00
C0004,Carl Court,carl@shop.com,-3,M,2024-02-02,45.00
C0005,Dana Diaz,,52,Female,2021-07-30,210.00
C0006,Evan Ellis,evan@shop.com,38,M,2099-01-01,15.00
C0007,Farah Khan,farah@shop.com,45,F,2023-03-03,60.00
C0007,Farah Khan,farah@shop.com,45,F,2023-03-03,60.00
C0008,Greg Green,greg@shop.com,60,Unknown,2022-09-09,N/A
C0009,Hana Ito,hana@shop.com,27,Male,2023-08-08,33.33
C0010,Ian Ives,ian@shop.com,,M,2020-12-12,17.00
```

criteria: Catches all the seeded defects: duplicate key (C0007), missing email (C0005), malformed email (C0003), negative age (C0004) and blank age (C0010), inconsistent gender coding, future date (C0006), non-numeric order_total (C0008)
Correctly assigns issues to data-quality dimensions (completeness, validity, uniqueness, consistency, accuracy)
Assigns sensible severity / prioritisation (e.g. duplicate key and non-numeric amount are load-breaking; a single blank age is minor)
Gives concrete, correct remediation for each issue (dedupe rule, format validation, range constraint, value standardisation, type coercion / reject-on-load)
Does not raise false positives on the clean rows (e.g. does not flag the legitimate 0.00 order_total as invalid without qualification), and notes downstream/analytics impact
